title: NetID - Adding or Changing a Recovery Email Address
body: Go to the NetID Modification Tool . Enter your NetID and Password and click Log in . You will need to authenticate with MFA-Duo. If you cannot log into your NetID see: NetID - Recovering a Forgotten NetID / NetID Account Recovery (Password Reset) Scroll down to Set Recovery Email : Enter the email address you wish to use and click Next . Important: Your recovery email address may not be a UW Madison (@wisc.edu or @<dept>.wisc.edu) email address. Valid email addresses include @gmail.com, @yahoo.com, @hotmail.com, etc. Additionally, a recovery email address can only belong to a single NetID . You will be unable to set a recovery email address that is already set as the recovery address of a different NetID. An email with a recovery code will be sent to the inbox of the recovery email address you submitted with subject NetID Account Recovery Code sent from NetID Administration <help@doit.wisc.edu> : Enter the 6-digit code in the Recovery Code box and click Next . After successfully confirmed your recovery email with the recovery code you'll be sent back to the NetID Modification tool with a green success banner: Error Messages: Error 1: Recovery email is in use by someone else This error means that your recovery email is already in use by someone else and you will need to enter a different email address for your recovery email. Error 2: Must provide new recovery email address This error means the recovery email address you entered is already in use for your NetID account. If you encounter any problems while setting your Recovery E-mail please contact the DoIT Help Desk . You may also wish to verify / change your Account Recovery questions: Changing Account Recovery Questions
